perturbation.original_text: 2. TERM AND PROTECTION PERIODS. This NDA shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of one (1) year unless sooner terminated in writing by either party. This NDA will automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods on the anniversary of the Effective Date, unless otherwise terminated in writing by either party. All obligations contained herein shall continue to bind the parties, their successors and permitted assigns and representatives, until the latter of (a) expiry or termination of this NDA or (b) three years from disclosure to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. TERM AND PROTECTION PERIODS. This NDA shall commence upon the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of one (1) year unless sooner terminated in writing by either party. All obligations contained herein shall continue to bind the parties, their successors and permitted assigns and representatives, until the latter of (a) expiry or termination of this NDA or (b) three years from disclosure to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'This NDA will automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods on the anniversary of the Effective Date, unless otherwise terminated in writing by either party.', it creates an in-text contradiction with the overall intention of the agreement to protect confidential information. While the agreement still outlines obligations after termination or expiry, the elimination of the automatic renewal clause reduces the defined term which can create unclarity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. At the Discloser’s request, the Recipient shall promptly return any or all Confidential Information received from the Discloser (including, without limitation, any summaries or copies or Confidential Information) or will certify through an officer of the Recipient that all Confidential Information received from the Discloser, and any summaries or copies thereof, have been destroyed. For greater certainty, failure of the Discloser to make such request of Recipient shall not entitle Recipient to make any further use of the Confidential Information or otherwise extend Recipient’s rights set forth herein after expiration or termination of this NDA and Recipient specifically agrees to cease any further use of Discloser’s Confidential Information in such event.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause for automatic renewal of the NDA, ensuring continued protection of confidential information. The changed text omits this renewal clause, which directly contradicts the 'RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' section. Without automatic renewal, the NDA term is limited to one year, potentially ending the protection of confidential information sooner than intended, and creating an inconsistency with the obligation to return or destroy confidential information even after expiration or termination. Therefore, the omission creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: b. Recipient shall protect the Confidential Information using the same degree of care that Recipient uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature to prevent the unauthorized use, reproduction or disclosure of the Confidential Information. Such care shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any employees, contractors, or third parties except to those employees, contractors or third parties who have a need to know the Confidential Information to fulfill the Purpose, provided that: (i) Recipient shall be vicariously liable for any act or omission of such employees, contractors or third parties respecting the Confidential Information, (ii) such employee, contractor or third party has been advised of the confidentiality obligations contained in this NDA and has either agreed in writing to be bound by the obligations of this NDA, or has entered into a binding written obligation of confidentiality with the Recipient that affords substantially similar protection of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Recipient shall protect the Confidential Information using a degree of care it deems appropriate. Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any employees, contractors, or third parties except to those employees, contractors or third parties who have a need to know the Confidential Information to fulfill the Purpose, provided that: (i) Recipient shall be vicariously liable for any act or omission of such employees, contractors or third parties respecting the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Such care shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.' and '(ii) such employee, contractor or third party has been advised of the confidentiality obligations contained in this NDA and has either agreed in writing to be bound by the obligations of this NDA, or has entered into a binding written obligation of confidentiality with the Recipient that affords substantially similar protection of the Confidential Information.', the clause becomes self-contradictory. Initially, the recipient must use reasonable care, but the omission undermines this by allowing a discretionary level of care. The deletion of the requirement for employees/contractors to agree to confidentiality creates a potential contradiction, as the 'vicariously liable' clause assumes there's an underlying confidentiality obligation which is no longer clearly mandated. Thus, this contradicts Section 8 as there could be inappropriate use due to this omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDIES. Recipient acknowledges that the Confidential Information has been developed at significant cost and has significant commercial value to Discloser, and Recipient agrees that disclosure or inappropriate use of the Confidential Information could cause Discloser irreparable harm. Recipient agrees therefore that Discloser will have the right to seek, in addition to any of its other rights and remedies under law and equity, injunctive relief for any violation of this NDA without posting bond or by posting bond at the lowest amount required by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the requirement for 'reasonable degree of care' in protecting confidential information and removes the obligation for employees/contractors to agree to confidentiality. This contradicts the 'REMEDIES' section, because the remedies are predicated on 'inappropriate use', and the omission of reasonable care and confidentiality agreements from employees/contractors now creates a scenario where inappropriate use may occur without violating the agreement, thereby undermining Discloser's right to seek remedies, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. NO RESTRICTIONS ON GENERAL KNOWLEDGE. The Participant acknowledges that Magnet Forensics may develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to their Confidential Information. Nothing in this NDA shall prohibit Magnet Forensics from developing products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Participant’s Confidential Information provided that Magnet Forensics does not violate any of its obligations under this NDA. The parties agree that because of exposure to Participant’s Confidential Information, employees of Magnet Forensics may gain or enhance their general knowledge, skills and experience (including ideas, concepts, know-how and techniques) related to Participant's business ("General Knowledge"). The subsequent use by these employees of such General Knowledge as retained in their unaided memories, without reference to Confidential Information in written, electronic or other fixed form, shall not constitute a breach of this NDA. Magnet Forensics shall have no obligation to limit or restrict the assignment of employees or to pay royalties for any work resulting from the use of such General Knowledge.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. NO RESTRICTIONS ON GENERAL KNOWLEDGE. The Participant acknowledges that Magnet Forensics may develop information internally, or receive information from other parties, that is similar to their Confidential Information. Nothing in this NDA shall prohibit Magnet Forensics from developing products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Participant’s Confidential Information. The parties agree that because of exposure to Participant’s Confidential Information, employees of Magnet Forensics may gain or enhance their general knowledge, skills and experience (including ideas, concepts, know-how and techniques) related to Participant's business ("General Knowledge"). The subsequent use by these employees of such General Knowledge as retained in their unaided memories, shall constitute a breach of this NDA. Magnet Forensics shall have no obligation to limit or restrict the assignment of employees or to pay royalties for any work resulting from the use of such General Knowledge.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase “provided that Magnet Forensics does not violate any of its obligations under this NDA” and changing "shall not constitute a breach of this NDA" to "shall constitute a breach of this NDA", the altered section 6 directly contradicts the intent of the entire agreement to protect confidential information. This creates ambiguity about what constitutes a violation and under what circumstances. Previously the agreement protected the use of general knowledge gained so long as no direct obligations were violated, and this change undermines the spirit of the entire NDA as it relates to general knowledge.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. a. Recipient shall hold Confidential Information in confidence and use, reproduce and disclose the Confidential Information only to the extent reasonably required to facilitate a business relationship between the parties (the “Purpose”). b. Recipient shall protect the Confidential Information using a degree of care it deems appropriate. Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any employees, contractors, or third parties except to those employees, contractors or third parties who have a need to know the Confidential Information to fulfill the Purpose, provided that: (i) Recipient shall be vicariously liable for any act or omission of such employees, contractors or third parties respecting the Confidential Information. c. Recipient agrees that it shall not alter, modify, adapt, create derivative works, translate, deface, decompile, disassemble, convert into human readable form, or reverse engineer all, or any part, of any materials to which it is provided access by Discloser. d. Each party will promptly advise the other in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information of which it may become aware.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 creates a direct contradiction with the 'CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS' section. The original text allowed the use of general knowledge gained by employees as long as the NDA obligations were not violated. The changed text states that the subsequent use of this 'General Knowledge' constitutes a breach of the NDA. This directly conflicts with the 'CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS' section, which outlines the permissible use and protection of confidential information, creating an inconsistency in what constitutes a breach and undermining the purpose of the NDA. The explanation states this as well, indicating the change undermines the spirit of the entire NDA as it relates to general knowledge.

perturbation.original_text: d. Each party will promptly advise the other in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information of which it may become aware.
perturbation.changed_text: d. Each party will advise the other in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information of which it may become aware.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "promptly" from section 3d, this introduces uncertainty to the time frame. This weakens the obligation to act quickly, possibly impacting the injured party's ability to resolve the conflict in a timely manner. The term is used multiple times through the NDA as this creates a contradicion in section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. At the Discloser’s request, the Recipient shall promptly return any or all Confidential Information received from the Discloser (including, without limitation, any summaries or copies or Confidential Information) or will certify through an officer of the Recipient that all Confidential Information received from the Discloser, and any summaries or copies thereof, have been destroyed. For greater certainty, failure of the Discloser to make such request of Recipient shall not entitle Recipient to make any further use of the Confidential Information or otherwise extend Recipient’s rights set forth herein after expiration or termination of this NDA and Recipient specifically agrees to cease any further use of Discloser’s Confidential Information in such event.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that parties must 'promptly advise' the other party in writing of any misappropriation. The changed text removes 'promptly,' introducing ambiguity regarding the timeframe for notification. The contradicted text uses the word 'promptly' to describe the return of confidential information. By removing 'promptly' from the changed text, a contradiction is introduced because section 5 expects an immediate action that is no longer enforced, as it would not necessarily be considered an immediate action anymore, leading to a contradiction. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information is and shall remain with the Discloser, and no rights to the Confidential Information are granted under this NDA other than the rights expressly granted in Section 3. Recipient shall not acquire any ownership right, interest or title in or to the Confidential Information or any intellectual property rights therein or the right to obtain or apply for such rights under this NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information is and shall remain with the Discloser, and no rights to the Confidential Information are granted under this NDA. Recipient shall not acquire any ownership right, interest or title in or to the Confidential Information or any intellectual property rights therein or the right to obtain or apply for such rights under this NDA.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "other than the rights expressly granted in Section 3" this clause in section 7 now creates a contradiction with section 3, because it provides that all rights remain with the Discloser while simultaneously stating that Recipient has no rights. However, section 3 grants rights to the recipient for use of Confidential Information to the extent reasonably required to facilitate a business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. a. Recipient shall hold Confidential Information in confidence and use, reproduce and disclose the Confidential Information only to the extent reasonably required to facilitate a business relationship between the parties (the “Purpose”). b. Recipient shall protect the Confidential Information using a degree of care it deems appropriate. Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any employees, contractors, or third parties except to those employees, contractors or third parties who have a need to know the Confidential Information to fulfill the Purpose, provided that: (i) Recipient shall be vicariously liable for any act or omission of such employees, contractors or third parties respecting the Confidential Information. c. Recipient agrees that it shall not alter, modify, adapt, create derivative works, translate, deface, decompile, disassemble, convert into human readable form, or reverse engineer all, or any part, of any materials to which it is provided access by Discloser. d. Each party will advise the other in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information of which it may become aware.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 7 explicitly states that no rights to the Confidential Information are granted under this NDA other than the rights expressly granted in Section 3. However, the changed text omits this exception, stating that Recipient shall not acquire any ownership right, interest or title in or to the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts section 3 which states the recipient shall hold Confidential Information in confidence and use, reproduce and disclose the Confidential Information only to the extent reasonably required to facilitate a business relationship between the parties. The omission creates a contradiction by stating the recipient has no rights regarding confidential information, while section 3 explicitly grants them rights related to use, reproduction and disclosure to the extent reasonably required. This is a strong contradiction.
